raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.7,"R1.2":0.7,"R1.3":0.7,"R1.4":0.3,"R1.5":0.7,"R1.6":0.7,"R1.7":0.3,"RX.1":1.0,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":1.0,"RX.5":1.0},"active_set":["R1.1","R1.2","R1.3","R1.5","R1.6","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"category_focus":"R1","clarification_needed":false,"clarification_question":"","rationale":{"R1.1":"The delegation asks to make the paper excerpt stronger, which centrally includes sharpening the thesis, contribution, and framing above the sentence level.","R1.2":"Strengthening the draft requires checking that the motivation, method, design choice, and claimed benefits form a coherent argument chain.","R1.3":"The excerpt relies on reported BLEU, terminology accuracy, and human preference results, so stronger writing must align claims with those stated results.","R1.5":"The artifact is a multi-paragraph paper passage whose persuasiveness may depend on section-level narrative ordering and exposition flow.","R1.6":"A request to make text stronger naturally includes improving sentence-level clarity, concision, register, and emphasis.","RX.1":"Any projection must preserve uncertainty about what kind of strengthening is intended because the delegation is broad.","RX.2":"The responsibility includes avoiding stronger claims than the reported evidence can support.","RX.3":"The agent must stay within projecting responsibilities rather than editing or rewriting the artifact.","RX.4":"The projection should avoid endorsing unsupported scientific or downstream deployment claims that could mislead readers.","RX.5":"The projection should keep responsibility assignments traceable to the delegation and the artifact’s explicit content."}}

rationale.R1.1: The delegation asks to make the paper excerpt stronger, which centrally includes sharpening the thesis, contribution, and framing above the sentence level.
rationale.R1.2: Strengthening the draft requires checking that the motivation, method, design choice, and claimed benefits form a coherent argument chain.
rationale.R1.3: The excerpt relies on reported BLEU, terminology accuracy, and human preference results, so stronger writing must align claims with those stated results.
rationale.R1.5: The artifact is a multi-paragraph paper passage whose persuasiveness may depend on section-level narrative ordering and exposition flow.
rationale.R1.6: A request to make text stronger naturally includes improving sentence-level clarity, concision, register, and emphasis.
rationale.RX.1: Any projection must preserve uncertainty about what kind of strengthening is intended because the delegation is broad.
rationale.RX.2: The responsibility includes avoiding stronger claims than the reported evidence can support.
rationale.RX.3: The agent must stay within projecting responsibilities rather than editing or rewriting the artifact.
rationale.RX.4: The projection should avoid endorsing unsupported scientific or downstream deployment claims that could mislead readers.
rationale.RX.5: The projection should keep responsibility assignments traceable to the delegation and the artifact’s explicit content.
